posts.raw_content:
This nation was in the midst of the worst presidency, pre Biden, in history. Gas shortages when there never needed to have been were prevalent. Inflation, unemployment, and interest were out of control. Under the peanut farmer, the misery index (inflation + unemployment) reached 21%. That broke down as 7.5% unemployment and 13.5% inflation. On Dec 20, 1980 (from memory) the prime lending rate exceeded 20%.

In November of 79, radical revolutionaries held our entire nation in submission as it took 66 American hostages and the feckless admin of Jimmy was powerless. Again prior to Biden, Jimmy was the weakest president of the past 100 years. Although, he was perhaps not nearly as corrupt as the more recent iterations of pathetic and catastrophic presidencies.

A song by Tony Orlando and Dawn (written about 4 years prior) became a rallying cry for America during the hostage crisis which played out on wooden or furniture console cathode ray tubes barely 20" diagonal, but in color. America was at a low point.

Those who remember this time period even if noninterventionist now, are likely to feel differently that those who do not about the current strikes on Iran.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Z8fhciUojQ0
